project: VERA: Verified Erasure under Reweighting Ambiguity
phase: claim-grade theorem coverage extension over candidate and group counts
purpose: Test the finite-family false-acceptance guarantee over an explicit grid of validation size, candidate-family size, validated-environment count, and delta. This study is independent of all real-data outcomes and cannot alter the confirmatory real-data protocol.

study.target_environment_offsets: For G environments, use G equally spaced offsets from -0.005 to 0.005; for G=1 use offset 0.
study.target_sampling: Within each cell, allocate floor(validation_size / environment_count) independent examples to every validated environment.
study.source_sampling: Within each cell, allocate floor(validation_size / 2) independent examples to each source class for every attacker.
study.selection_rule: Deploy the lowest-index candidate whose target and balanced-leakage certificates all pass; abstain if none pass.
study.multiplicity: Use candidate-wise intersection-union tests with local alpha delta/candidate_count. Split local alpha equally between the two class-conditional recalls in each balanced-leakage component and between positive and negative paired-harm probability bounds. No division over constraints inside a candidate is needed for an intersection-union test.
study.pass_condition: For every one of 6 x 4 x 3 x 3 cells, the simultaneous one-sided 95% Clopper-Pearson upper bound on Monte Carlo false acceptance is at most the cell delta. Every cell must include at least one truly contract-violating candidate, and all 216 cells must be reported regardless of outcome.
study.stopping_rule: Run all 216 cells with exactly 2000 replicates. No probability, threshold, grid, seed, or pass-condition change is allowed after this file is committed.
